stem: The function \( f(x)= [[a]]x^{2} \) for \( x\le [[p]] \) and \( f(x)=mx+b \) for \( x>[[p]] \) is differentiable at \( x=[[p]] \). What is \( m \)?

The function \( f(x)= [[a]]x^{2}+[[e]] \) for \( x\le [[p]] \) and \( f(x)=m(x-[[p]])+b \) for \( x>[[p]] \) is both continuous and differentiable at \( x=[[p]] \), where \( m \) is chosen to match the slope. What is \( b \)?
solution: Continuity at \( x=[[p]] \) requires the two pieces to agree there: \( b = f([[p]]) = [[a]]\cdot[[p]]^{2}+[[e]] = [[answer]] \) (the slope-matching condition on \( m \) does not affect \( b \)).
distractor_rationales: This is the slope \( m=f'(p) \), not the value \( b=f(p) \).
This drops the constant term \( e \) from \( f(p) \).
This uses only the constant term, ignoring the quadratic contribution at \( x=p \).
This adds an extra, unjustified term \( p \) to the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: The function \( f(x)=[[a]]x^{2}+[[b]]x \) for \( x\le [[p]] \) and \( f(x)=[[c]]x^{2}+kx+d \) for \( x>[[p]] \) is differentiable at \( x=[[p]] \) (with \( d \) chosen so \( f \) is also continuous there). What is \( k \)?
distractor_rationales: This uses only the left piece's slope, forgetting to subtract the right piece's quadratic contribution.
This drops the linear term \( b \) from the left piece's slope.
This adds instead of subtracts the right piece's quadratic slope term.
This omits the left piece's quadratic contribution \( 2ap \).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: The function \( f(x)=[[a]]x^{2}+[[e]] \) for \( x\le [[p]] \) and \( f(x)=mx+b \) for \( x>[[p]] \) is both continuous and differentiable at \( x=[[p]] \). Solve the resulting system for \( m \) and \( b \); what is \( b \)?
solution: Differentiability gives \( m=2[[a]][[p]] \); substituting into the continuity equation \( m[[p]]+b=[[a]][[p]]^{2}+[[e]] \) and solving gives \( b = [[a]][[p]]^{2}+[[e]]-2[[a]][[p]]\cdot[[p]] = -[[a]][[p]]^{2}+[[e]] \), which simplifies to \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reports the slope \( m \) instead of the intercept \( b \).
This forgets to subtract the extra \( mp \) term when solving for \( b \).
This drops the quadratic contribution entirely.
This flips the sign of the quadratic term.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
